title: InMySkin
contributors: Guido Sijabat, Ana Paola, Petr Mogutov
oneLiner: A VR experience that lets you see through someone else’s eyes, simulating how the world feels under different visual conditions.
summaryShort: InMySkin is a virtual reality experience that simulates altered visual conditions, allowing users to perceive the world through the lens of visual impairments.
summaryMedium: InMySkin is a virtual reality experience that simulates altered visual conditions, allowing users to perceive the world through the lens of visual impairments. By immersing users in different perceptual states, the project transforms vision from a passive sense into an active, embodied experience of limitation, distortion, and adaptation. Vision is often assumed to be stable and universal, yet millions of people experience the world through conditions such as glaucoma, cataracts, or color blindness. These realities are difficult to communicate through static images or descriptions alone, creating a gap in empathy and understanding. This project emerges from the need to make invisible conditions perceptible, using immersive technology to bridge that experiential gap.
summaryLong: InMySkin is a virtual reality experience that simulates altered visual conditions, allowing users to perceive the world through the lens of visual impairments. By immersing users in different perceptual states, the project transforms vision from a passive sense into an active, embodied experience of limitation, distortion, and adaptation. Vision is often assumed to be stable and universal, yet millions of people experience the world through conditions such as glaucoma, cataracts, or color blindness. These realities are difficult to communicate through static images or descriptions alone, creating a gap in empathy and understanding. This project emerges from the need to make invisible conditions perceptible, using immersive technology to bridge that experiential gap. The project reframes empathy as something that can be experienced rather than explained. By simulating different visual conditions in real time, InMySkin allows users to inhabit alternative perceptual realities. Each mode of vision alters clarity, color, and field of view, revealing how drastically perception shapes interaction with the environment. The experience highlights that what we consider “normal” vision is only one version of many possible ways of seeing. InMySkin is a virtual reality experience that simulates altered visual conditions, allowing users to perceive the world through the lens of visual impairments. By immersing users in different perceptual states, the project transforms vision from a passive sense into an active, embodied experience of limitation, distortion, and adaptation. The system was developed as a VR experience using Unity, integrating real-time visual filters that simulate conditions such as glaucoma (tunnel vision), cataracts (blur and opacity), and color blindness (reduced or shifted color perception). The experience is deployed on VR headsets, allowing users to navigate environments while switching between different visual modes. Iterations focused on balancing realism and usability, ensuring that the simulations are both accurate enough to convey the conditions and intuitive enough to remain navigable. The result is an immersive tool for education, awareness, and embodied understanding.
